name: Oxygen

dosing: High-flow oxygen at 15-25 LPM via non-rebreather mask at attack onset
Many users find 15 LPM sufficient, while others need 25 LPM for stubborn attacks
Hospital and ER settings may use unspecified higher flow rates
preparations: A non-rebreather mask is standard, though specialized masks like the ClusterO2 kit or OptiMask improve effectiveness by creating negative sinus pressure
Home setups typically use medical-grade oxygen tanks from a home medical supplier, or welding oxygen as a more accessible alternative
Portable E-tanks allow on-the-go use but deplete quickly at high flow rates
schedule: Begin breathing oxygen at the first sign of an attack and continue for 10-20 minutes until pain fully subsides
Continue oxygen for several minutes after the pain stops to prevent rebound attacks
Many users combine oxygen with an energy drink at onset for faster relief

sideEffects: Rebound attack if oxygen is stopped too soon
Effectiveness may decrease over a prolonged cycle
Difficulty obtaining a prescription from doctors unfamiliar with CH
Cost burden when insurance won't cover
Limited portability restricts use outside the home
